officialRules: IBS score shooting consists of five record targets and one sighter target.
Each record shot is scored separately.
The highest scoring ring touched determines shot value.
A center dot or X is scored as 10X.
Five record targets determine a match score with highest possible score 50-5X.
courseOfFire: Use the sighter target as needed.
Fire one scored shot on each record target.
instructionAuthority.intendedUse: Benchrest precision score shooting, measuring accurate placement on individual bulls.
passFailOrScoringRules: Highest score wins in score shooting.

aimPointRules: Aim at each record bull for the scored shot.
Sighter target may be used as needed.
scoringZones: Rings and center X are known conceptually; exact dimensions are UNKNOWN / REQUIRES OFFICIAL SOURCE.

evidenceRequirements.requiredAimPoint: one per scored bull or derived from official bull centers
minimumConfidenceRequirements: Official target geometry required before authoritative ring score.
Each impact must map to the correct record bull.

unknowns.neededSource: Official IBS 100 yard rimfire rulebook or target rule source.
Official IBS target dimensions for rings, X dot, bull centers, and sheet layout.
Physical IBS 100 yard rimfire target artwork or official target supplier specification.

sourceAudit.notes: IBS discipline summary describes group and score shooting formats.
IBS identifies National Target Company as authorized supplier; detailed dimensions require supplier/official target source.
